name: SketchUpModel
summary: A SketchUp Model entity. This is an instance of a SketchUp Model that is inserted into a .layout file. You can change the render mode, line weight, and set the current scene for the SketchUp Model with this interface.

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: The #initialize method creates a new Layout::SketchUpModel.
constructors.remarks: The #initialize method creates a new Layout::SketchUpModel.

throws.when: if path does not point to a valid SketchUp Model file

methods.name: camera_modified?
methods.summary: The #camera_modified? method returns whether the camera of the Layout::SketchUpModel has been modified.
methods.remarks: The #camera_modified? method returns whether the camera of the Layout::SketchUpModel has been modified.

methods.name: current_scene_modified?
methods.summary: The #current_scene_modified? method returns whether the most recently selected scene of the Layout::SketchUpModel has been modified.
methods.remarks: The #current_scene_modified? method returns whether the most recently selected scene of the Layout::SketchUpModel has been modified.

throws.when: if the most recently selected scene no longer exists

methods.name: display_background?
methods.summary: The #display_background? method returns whether the background is displayed for the Layout::SketchUpModel.
methods.remarks: The #display_background? method returns whether the background is displayed for the Layout::SketchUpModel.

methods.name: effects_modified?
methods.summary: The #effects_modified? method returns whether the shadow or fog settings of the Layout::SketchUpModel have been modified.
methods.remarks: The #effects_modified? method returns whether the shadow or fog settings of the Layout::SketchUpModel have been modified.

methods.name: entities
methods.summary: The #entities method returns the Group that represents the Layout::SketchUpModel in its exploded form.
methods.remarks: The #entities method returns the Group that represents the Layout::SketchUpModel in its exploded form. The Group will contain a Image for raster and hybrid-rendered models, and will contain a Group of LayOut entities for vector and hybrid-rendered models.

methods.name: layers_modified?
methods.summary: Note: In SketchUp 2020, SketchUp “layers” were renamed to “tags”.
methods.remarks: Note: In SketchUp 2020, SketchUp “layers” were renamed to “tags”. For consistency with the SketchUp API, this will continue to refer to “tags” as “layers”. The #layers_modified? method returns whether the layers of the Layout::SketchUpModel has been modified.

methods.name: model_to_paper_point
methods.summary: The #model_to_paper_point method converts the Geom::Point3d in the Layout::SketchUpModel to a Geom::Point2d in paper space.
methods.remarks: The #model_to_paper_point method converts the Geom::Point3d in the Layout::SketchUpModel to a Geom::Point2d in paper space.

methods.name: output_entities
methods.summary: The #output_entities method returns the Group that represents the Layout::SketchUpModel in its exported form.
methods.remarks: The #output_entities method returns the Group that represents the Layout::SketchUpModel in its exported form. The Group will contain a Image for raster and hybrid-rendered models, and will contain a Group of LayOut entities for vector and hybrid-rendered models. This takes into account the output resolution set in the document's PageInfo, and the render mode override set on the document.

methods.name: perspective?
methods.summary: The #perspective? method returns whether the Layout::SketchUpModel's view is perspective or orthographic.
methods.remarks: The #perspective? method returns whether the Layout::SketchUpModel's view is perspective or orthographic.

methods.name: preserve_scale_on_resize?
methods.summary: The #preserve_scale_on_resize? method returns whether the scale is preserved when the Layout::SketchUpModel is resized.
methods.remarks: The #preserve_scale_on_resize? method returns whether the scale is preserved when the Layout::SketchUpModel is resized.

methods.name: render
methods.remarks: The #render method renders the Layout::SketchUpModel. If the model belongs to a Document, then the render will be performed at the quality set in document.page_info (see Document and PageInfo). Otherwise, the render will be performed at Low quality.

throws.when: if the Layout::SketchUpModel is on a locked Layer

methods.name: render_needed?
methods.summary: The #render_needed? method returns whether the Layout::SketchUpModel needs to be rendered.
methods.remarks: The #render_needed? method returns whether the Layout::SketchUpModel needs to be rendered.

methods.name: reset_camera
methods.summary: The #reset_camera method resets the Layout::SketchUpModel's camera to the scene's setting.
methods.remarks: The #reset_camera method resets the Layout::SketchUpModel's camera to the scene's setting.

throws.when: if the Layout::SketchUpModel is on a locked Layer

methods.name: reset_effects
methods.summary: The #reset_effects method resets the Layout::SketchUpModel's shadow and fog settings to the scene's settings.
methods.remarks: The #reset_effects method resets the Layout::SketchUpModel's shadow and fog settings to the scene's settings.

throws.when: if the Layout::SketchUpModel is on a locked Layer

methods.name: reset_layers
methods.summary: Note: In SketchUp 2020, SketchUp “layers” were renamed to “tags”.
methods.remarks: Note: In SketchUp 2020, SketchUp “layers” were renamed to “tags”. For consistency with the SketchUp API, this will continue to refer to “tags” as “layers”. The #reset_layers method resets the Layout::SketchUpModel's layers to the scene's setting.

throws.when: if the Layout::SketchUpModel is on a locked Layer

methods.name: reset_style
methods.summary: The #reset_style method resets the Layout::SketchUpModel's style to the scene's setting.
methods.remarks: The #reset_style method resets the Layout::SketchUpModel's style to the scene's setting.

throws.when: if the Layout::SketchUpModel is on a locked Layer

methods.name: scenes
methods.summary: The #scenes method returns an array of scene names that are available for the Layout::SketchUpModel.
methods.remarks: The #scenes method returns an array of scene names that are available for the Layout::SketchUpModel. The first scene will always be the default scene, called “Last saved SketchUp View”.

methods.name: style_modified?
methods.summary: The #style_modified? method returns whether the style of the Layout::SketchUpModel has been modified.
methods.remarks: The #style_modified? method returns whether the style of the Layout::SketchUpModel has been modified.

properties.name: clip_mask
properties.summary: The #clip_mask method returns the clip mask entity for the Layout::SketchUpModel, or nil if it does not have one.
properties.remarks: The #clip_mask method returns the clip mask entity for the Layout::SketchUpModel, or nil if it does not have one. clip_mask can be a Rectangle, Ellipse, or Path.

properties.name: current_scene
properties.summary: The #current_scene method returns the index of the most recently selected scene of the Layout::SketchUpModel.
properties.remarks: The #current_scene method returns the index of the most recently selected scene of the Layout::SketchUpModel.

properties.name: dash_scale
properties.summary: The #dash_scale method returns the dash scale for the Layout::SketchUpModel.
properties.remarks: The #dash_scale method returns the dash scale for the Layout::SketchUpModel. A scale value of 0.0 means the dashes are scaled based on the line weight.

properties.name: display_background
properties.summary: The #display_background= method sets whether the background is displayed for the Layout::SketchUpModel.
properties.remarks: The #display_background= method sets whether the background is displayed for the Layout::SketchUpModel.

properties.name: line_weight
properties.summary: The #line_weight method returns the line weight for the Layout::SketchUpModel.
properties.remarks: The #line_weight method returns the line weight for the Layout::SketchUpModel.

properties.name: perspective
properties.summary: The #perspective= method sets whether the Layout::SketchUpModel's view is perspective or orthographic.
properties.remarks: The #perspective= method sets whether the Layout::SketchUpModel's view is perspective or orthographic.

properties.name: preserve_scale_on_resize
properties.summary: The #preserve_scale_on_resize= method sets whether the scale is preserved when the Layout::SketchUpModel is resized.
properties.remarks: The #preserve_scale_on_resize= method sets whether the scale is preserved when the Layout::SketchUpModel is resized.

properties.name: render_mode
properties.summary: The #render_mode method returns the render mode of the Layout::SketchUpModel.
properties.remarks: The #render_mode method returns the render mode of the Layout::SketchUpModel. The render mode can be one of the following: Layout::SketchUpModel::RASTER_RENDER Layout::SketchUpModel::HYBRID_RENDER Layout::SketchUpModel::VECTOR_RENDER

properties.name: scale
properties.summary: The #scale method returns the scale of the Layout::SketchUpModel.
properties.remarks: The #scale method returns the scale of the Layout::SketchUpModel.

properties.name: view
properties.summary: The #view method returns the standard view of the Layout::SketchUpModel.
properties.remarks: The #view method returns the standard view of the Layout::SketchUpModel. The standard view can be one of the following values: Layout::SketchUpModel::CUSTOM_VIEW Layout::SketchUpModel::TOP_VIEW Layout::SketchUpModel::TOP_RELATIVE_VIEW Layout::SketchUpModel::BOTTOM_VIEW Layout::SketchUpModel::BOTTOM_RELATIVE_VIEW Layout::SketchUpModel::FRONT_VIEW Layout::SketchUpModel::BACK_VIEW Layout::SketchUpModel::LEFT_VIEW Layout::SketchUpModel::RIGHT_VIEW Layout::SketchUpModel::ISO_VIEW
